---
title: "LRPDID03: Waitlist-crossover (DiD) ITT effect on phoneme blending (B)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
did_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "did_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPDID03 is the **waitlist-crossover / difference-in-differences** analysis for
phoneme blending (`blending`) - a within-person replication of the randomised ITT effect
estimated by LRPITT08. It stacks the two early periods (P1 = t1->t2, P2 = t2->t3)
for both arms; each child is their **own control** (a child random intercept), and
the immediate arm - treated in both periods - anchors the time/maturation trend.

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

The waitlist arm is the **only untreated cell** (P1) and crosses to the
intervention in P2. The treatment coefficient is a **difference-in-differences**:
the waitlist's P1->P2 gain jump *over and above* the immediate arm's P1->P2 change.
It is identified jointly by the period-1 between-arm contrast and the waitlist's own
crossover, with stable child traits differenced out by the random intercept.

It is **not** a second randomised experiment: the P2 contrast is not randomised, so
identification leans on the parallel-trends assumption and the immediate arm as an
(imperfect, also-treated) time anchor. Read it as **triangulation** of the RCT, not
an independent replication; the two share the waitlist-P1 untreated cell.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Sign convention and the ceiling"}

Positive => the intervention helps. The Beta-Binomial **logit** link absorbs
ceiling effects: an immediate-arm child near the top of a bounded test makes a
small P2 gain *expected*, not a spurious negative time trend - which is exactly the
failure mode of a raw-gain DiD on the count scale.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
           + \delta\,\text{Treated}_{i,p}
           + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(B_{\mathrm{pre},i,p}, N)
           + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i,p} + u_{\text{child},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood on the period post-count, $N = 10$. ``Treated`` is
1 when the child is receiving the intervention that period (immediate: both;
waitlist: P2 only). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=32%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Crossover / DiD treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
did_summary
```

- `delta_*`: the difference-in-differences treatment effect on the logit scale
  (mean, equal-tailed central interval at coverage `ci_prob`).
- `delta_items_*`: the average marginal effect on the test-item scale (toggling
  ``Treated`` 0 -> 1 across the fitted rows), comparable to the ITT `tau_*` items.
- `beta_period_*`: the period (time/maturation) anchor from the immediate arm.
- `prob_*_pos`: posterior probability the coefficient is positive.
- For the **dose** variant the key coefficient is `beta_dose` (effect per 1 SD of
  intervention sessions) and no items translation is produced.

Compare `delta` (or `beta_dose`) against the randomised single-outcome effect in
LRPITT08: agreement is the triangulation this model is for.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
